ref: God made two powerful lights, the brighter one to rule the day and the other to rule the night. He also made the stars.
text: Tete Mawu wɔ nu keklẽ gã eveawo. Ewɔ gãtɔ, bena woaɖu ŋkeke dzi, kple suetɔ, bena woaɖu zã dzi, hekpe ɖe ɣletiviwo ŋu.
translated: In the beginning, God created two great things. He created the first, so that it rules over the day, and the second, so that it rules over the night, and also the stars.

ref: Please accept these as gifts I brought to you. God has been good to me, and I have everything I need.” Jacob kept insisting until Esau agreed.
text: Xa nye nusi wotsɔ vɛ na wò la dzro, elabena Yehowa yram, mekpɔ nu geɖe! Eye wótèe ɖe ŋu, vaseɖe esime wòxɔe.
translated: But I have trusted in your mercy; my heart will rejoice in your salvation. I will sing to Jehovah, because he has rewarded me.

ref: So Abraham buried his wife Sarah in Machpelah Cave that was in the field
text: Esia megbe la Abraham ɖi srɔ̃a Sara ɖe kpeto, si le Maxpela-gblea me la me le Mamre kasa; esia enye Hebron le Kanaan-nyigba dzi.
translated: After this, Abraham buried Sarah in the cave of the field of Machpelah, which is near Mamre; that is Hebron in the land of Canaan.
